notes:
All 5 Phase-5 tasks (mxdx-awe.21, .22, .23, .24, .25) executed and closed on branch brains/rust-p2p-interactive. The phase ships the core P2PTransport state machine, public API + native driver loop, idle watchdog, Ed25519-signed Verifying handshake (P0 security-critical), and a comprehensive test suite — the integration layer that ties Phase 1's P2PCrypto+Megolm, Phase 2's TurnCredentials+TurnRefreshTask, Phase 3's WebRtcChannel, and Phase 4's m.call.* signaling together into a single lifecycle-managed component.

T-50 (mxdx-awe.21, commit 934a3fe): crates/mxdx-p2p/src/transport/state.rs defines the 9-state P2PState enum (Idle, FetchingTurn, Inviting, Answering, Glare, Connecting, Verifying, Open, Failed) and the pure transition(state, event) -> TransitionResult function. The state enum does NOT hold Box<dyn WebRtcChannel> or P2PCrypto — those live in the driver, so the state remains Clone-able and trivially testable. Event enum aggregates all inbound sources (CallEventReceived, ChannelEvent, OutboundPressure, IdleTick, InviteTimeout, IceTimeout, TurnRefreshed, TurnExpired, VerifyOk, VerifyFail, DecryptStorm, Start, Hangup, RetryReady). Command enum models every side effect (SendInvite/Answer/Candidates/Hangup/SelectAnswer, StartVerifying, BeginFetchTurn, ConfigureIceServers, TearDownChannel, ResetIdle, DrainOutbound, EmitTelemetry, Schedule, LockoutDevice). TransitionResult::Illegal returned for unexpected (state, event) pairs — driver logs warn and keeps current state. ChannelEvent gains #[derive(Clone)] so the pure transition fn can accept an owned clone. 35 table-driven unit tests in src/transport/state.rs cover 26 happy-path rows across all 9 states + 9 illegal-transition rows + 5 sanity-check rows.

T-51 (mxdx-awe.22, commit 595abc0): crates/mxdx-p2p/src/transport/mod.rs exports SendOutcome + P2PStateSnapshot + outbound/inbound queue-depth constants (256 each per storm §4.4) + Megolm<Bytes> re-export. driver.rs implements DriverTask + P2PTransport public handle + P2PTransportConfig. Single tokio::select! loop (biased: shutdown, idle, control, call_event, outbound) handles all inputs. try_send is NON-BLOCKING by construction — regular sync fn, cheap state-snapshot mutex check, single try_send on bounded mpsc, returns FallbackToMatrix on not-Open OR queue-full. state() returns a cheap P2PStateSnapshot clone (no awaiting). Driver integrates Phase 1 P2PCrypto (for outbound encrypt via drain_outbound), tracks recent_nonces (REPLAY_WINDOW_SIZE=16) for replay detection, locked_peers (HashSet) for 3-strike lockout, verify_failures (HashMap<peer, count>) for per-peer 3-strike counting, last_decrypt_failures (VecDeque<Instant>) for storm §4.4 3/sec rate limit. mxdx-matrix + tracing added as native-only deps; ed25519-dalek added as cross-target dep (pure Rust, wasm-compatible). Driver traces Command::SendInvite etc. at info! during Phase 5; Phase 6 replaces with real Matrix sends through the caller-supplied MatrixClient. restart_ice handling documented: on RestartIceUnsupported (Phase-3 limitation), driver tears down + re-invites; try_send during the gap returns FallbackToMatrix (follow-up bead mxdx-clr tracks Phase-6 wiring).

T-52 (mxdx-awe.23, commit 595abc0): crates/mxdx-p2p/src/transport/idle.rs implements IdleWatchdog::spawn/reset/shutdown. Spawn creates a tokio task that loops { select! { control_rx -> reset OR shutdown, sleep(window) -> fire IdleTick then exit } }. Fresh sleep created each iteration so reset() effectively restarts the timer. Wired into driver: spawned on Open entry (Command::ResetIdle path), dropped on any transition away from Open (via was_open check in dispatch). 3 virtual-time tests with tokio::test(start_paused=true) + advance + yield_many helper: fires after window, reset prevents fire, shutdown cancels task.

T-53 (mxdx-awe.24, commit 3cf5304) — P0 SECURITY-CRITICAL: crates/mxdx-p2p/src/transport/verify.rs implements the storm §3.1 Ed25519-signed transcript handshake.
- DOMAIN_SEPARATION_TAG = b'mxdx.p2p.verify.v1' (versioned ASCII constant).
- build_transcript(): canonical offerer-first byte layout: domain_sep || 0x00 || room_id || 0x00 || session_uuid || 0x00 || call_id || offerer_nonce (32B) || answerer_nonce (32B) || 0x00 || offerer_party_id || 0x00 || answerer_party_id || 0x00 || offerer_sdp_fingerprint || 0x00 || answerer_sdp_fingerprint. 0x00 separators disambiguate variable-length UTF-8 string fields; nonces are fixed-width and carry no separator between themselves (avoids ambiguity since nonce bytes can legitimately be 0x00).
- canonical_ordering(): pure fn that rearranges (our, their) → (offerer, answerer) based on we_are_offerer flag determined by glare::resolve (Phase 4) + invite-state. Both peers produce BYTE-IDENTICAL transcripts despite occupying different roles.
- extract_sdp_fingerprint(): parses 'a=fingerprint:sha-256 AA:BB:...' lines, upper-case normalizes, picks lexicographically smallest when multiple (rare RTP+RTCP split case). Non-sha256 algorithms ignored.
- generate_nonce(): 32 bytes from aes_gcm::aead::OsRng.fill_bytes — same CSPRNG used by SealedKey::random and P2PCrypto IV generation. NOT rand::thread_rng, NOT a seeded PRNG.
- verify_peer_signature(): ed25519-dalek 2.x VerifyingKey::from_bytes + Verifier::verify over transcript bytes.
- HandshakeSigner + HandshakePeerKeySource traits for Phase 6 integration; EphemeralKeySigner + InMemoryPeerKeySource provided for tests.
- verify_handshake_response(): orchestration API. Decodes wire (signature, signer_pk, device_id); looks up Matrix-known public key for the peer device via HandshakePeerKeySource; REJECTS with PeerKeyMismatch if wire_pk != matrix_pk (closes the attack where adversary signs with their own key and claims to be the peer); then verifies against the transcript. Returns HandshakeOutcome enum mapping to state::VerifyFailureReason.
- HandshakeMsg serde enum: Challenge { nonce_b64, device_id } + Response { signature_b64, signer_ed25519_b64, device_id }.
- 35 new unit tests in src/transport/verify.rs: transcript determinism + 9 'differs on <field>' tests + sign/verify happy-path + tampered-transcript + wrong-signer + invalid-pk + base64 roundtrips + canonical-ordering-byte-identical-across-peers + ephemeral-signer-sign-verify + peer-key-mismatch simulation + handshake flow integration.
- 10 new integration tests in tests/verify_handshake.rs: correct signature verifies, wrong signer rejected (PeerKeyMismatch), unknown device rejected, invalid-payload rejection (malformed base64, challenge-in-response slot), modified transcript rejection, deterministic challenge builder, glare-winner roundtrip, b64 roundtrip, replay policy layering documentation.

T-54 (mxdx-awe.25, commit 159a490): crates/mxdx-p2p/tests/state_machine.rs adds 29 integration-level tests covering storm §3.1–§4.5:
- 3 happy-path reachability tests (full offerer + full answerer flows to Open, state_transition telemetry per transition).
- 8 failure-taxonomy tests (VerifyFail never reaches Open [property], SignatureMismatch → Failed + VerifyFailure telemetry, ReplayDetected → ReplayDetected telemetry, DeviceLockedOut → WrongPeer telemetry, InviteTimeout/IceTimeout/TurnExpired/DecryptStorm paths).
- 3 TURN refresh tests (Open + TurnRefreshed → ConfigureIceServers, FetchingTurn + TurnExpired → Failed, out-of-order VerifyOk in FetchingTurn → never reaches Open).
- 4 recovery tests (RetryReady after/before deadline, Start from Failed → FetchingTurn, peer invite from Failed → Answering).
- 3 idle-timeout tests (IdleTick tear-down, Message/OutboundPressure emit ResetIdle).
- 2 hangup tests (hangup from any active state → Idle, hangup emits SendHangup + TearDownChannel).
- 2 glare tests (concurrent invite → Glare, Glare state is pure no-op).
- 2 panic-freedom tests (8 states × 15 events grid sweep, illegal transitions never reach Open).
- 1 select_answer passthrough test.

SECURITY INVARIANTS VERIFIED:
- Transcript covers every storm §3.1 field (domain_sep, room_id, session_uuid, call_id, both nonces, both party_ids, both SDP fingerprints) in canonical order. 9 'differs on' tests lock the contract.
- Ed25519 via ed25519-dalek 2.x (industry-standard, audited, pure-Rust, wasm-compatible). Not custom keypair.
- Nonces from aes_gcm::aead::OsRng (CSPRNG, not seeded PRNG).
- Replay detection: DriverTask::check_replay tracks last 16 (nonce, call_id) pairs; unit test proves duplicate rejected; integration test documents layering (crypto layer is stateless by design — driver is the freshness enforcer).
- 3-strike device lockout: DriverTask::bump_verify_failure increments per-peer counter; third failure inserts into locked_peers HashSet and emits WrongPeer telemetry; subsequent start() with that peer short-circuits (driver check in handle_control).
- Megolm<Bytes> integrity: try_send takes Megolm<MatrixBytes> by value; Megolm's pub(crate) constructor inside mxdx-matrix::crypto_envelope is unchanged by Phase 5. No public constructor added. Phase 1 trybuild tests still enforce compile-time un-constructibility.
- scripts/check-no-unencrypted-sends.sh remains green throughout.
- PeerKeyMismatch protection: verify_handshake_response NEVER trusts the wire-carried signer_ed25519_b64 verbatim — compares to Matrix-known key via HandshakePeerKeySource; wire/Matrix divergence → PeerKeyMismatch (documented in tests/verify_handshake.rs::wrong_signer_is_rejected_with_peer_key_mismatch).

NPM DIVERGENCE (KNOWN, FOLLOW-UP FILED):
- npm's packages/core/p2p-transport.js uses a simple nonce ping-pong (peer_verify + peer_verify_response) in plaintext JSON over WebRTC DTLS only — no Ed25519 signature, no transcript.
- Rust T-53 ships the STRONGER storm-spec handshake. Wire-format divergence per ADR 2026-04-16-coordinated-rust-npm-releases.md requires coordinated release.
- Rust↔Rust P2P works immediately; Rust↔npm P2P at Verifying falls back to Matrix (which PRESERVES the cardinal E2EE invariant — no security regression, just no P2P benefit until npm upgrades).
- Follow-up bead mxdx-fqt files the npm-side upgrade to be delivered in a coordinated release pairing with Phase 6 worker/client wiring.

PHASE 6 INTEGRATION READY: HandshakeSigner + HandshakePeerKeySource traits defined with EphemeralKeySigner + InMemoryPeerKeySource for tests. Phase 6 (mxdx-btk follow-up) provides Matrix-backed impls via matrix-sdk's Olm machine (sign_with_device_key) and crypto store (get_peer_device_ed25519).

PHASE-1 FOLLOW-UP mxdx-2oi: storm §3.2 signature refinement — send_megolm(room_id, event_type, Megolm<Bytes>) takes event_type argument (not in storm spec pseudocode). Phase 5 did not touch this; it remains a Phase-6 integration decision per the original bead description.

follow_ups.title: npm-side upgrade: Ed25519-signed Verifying handshake (coordinated release)
follow_ups.reason: Wire-format divergence from Phase 5 T-53 Rust handshake. Requires coordinated release per ADR 2026-04-16-coordinated-rust-npm-releases.md. Until npm ships, Rust↔npm P2P at Verifying falls back to Matrix (which preserves the cardinal E2EE invariant, so no security regression — just no P2P benefit).

follow_ups.title: Wire Matrix-backed HandshakeSigner + PeerKeySource impls (Phase 6 integration)
follow_ups.reason: Phase 5 T-53 ships the traits + EphemeralKeySigner + InMemoryPeerKeySource for tests. Production impls bind to matrix-sdk's Olm machine (our side) and crypto store (peer side). Phase 6 owns this.

follow_ups.title: Wire restart_ice TURN-refresh tear-down + re-invite fallback
follow_ups.reason: Phase 3 marker documented that datachannel-sys 0.23 returns RestartIceUnsupported. Phase 5 T-51 documents the intended tear-down + re-invite flow but does NOT wire it at the ConfigureIceServers handler. Phase 6 integration owns this — measure the user-visible gap and file follow-up if > 2s.

verification_results.cargo_check_wasm: clean — no new warnings, no matrix-sdk pulled into wasm dep graph (matrix-sdk is native-only dep)
verification_results.cargo_check_workspace: clean — no new errors or warnings in downstream crates
verification_results.security_grep_gate: green — no forbidden patterns (send_raw | skip_encryption | unencrypted) in crates/mxdx-p2p
verification_results.cargo_clippy: no new errors. Pre-existing warnings in turn.rs:372 (result_unit_err, deferred) and trivial stylistic nits. New lib warnings: 4 (unused_imports in verify.rs tests — harmless). test-suite warnings: 12 (5 dup).

key_decisions: State enum does NOT hold Box<dyn WebRtcChannel> or P2PCrypto — those live in the driver's DriverTask struct so the state remains Clone-able and the pure transition function is trivially testable. This lets table-driven unit tests enumerate state × event pairs without constructing real backends.

try_send is a regular synchronous fn (not async) with a cheap state-snapshot mutex + a single mpsc::try_send. Non-blocking by construction, not just convention. The latency-critical path never awaits on the driver.

Driver event enum uses Event::ChannelEvent(BackendEvent) with a type alias import (BackendEvent = channel::ChannelEvent) to avoid the name collision between the Event variant and the channel::ChannelEvent type. This is the cleanest way without renaming one or the other.

Verifying handshake divergence from npm is a FEATURE, not a bug: the Rust version is the storm-spec (stronger) handshake. Matrix fallback preserves the cardinal E2EE invariant during the migration window. Coordinated release is tracked via mxdx-fqt follow-up.

PeerKeyMismatch protection — the wire-carried signer_ed25519_b64 is NEVER trusted verbatim; verification compares to the Matrix-device-known key via HandshakePeerKeySource. This closes the 'attacker signs with their own key and claims to be peer' attack cleanly.

Canonical offerer-first transcript ordering: both peers produce byte-identical transcripts despite occupying asymmetric roles. Determined by a we_are_offerer flag the driver computes from glare::resolve + invite state.

Replay detection is layered at DriverTask, NOT in verify_handshake_response — the crypto layer is stateless by design; the driver owns freshness enforcement via recent_nonces VecDeque<([u8;32], String)> bounded at 16 entries.

3-strike device lockout is layered at DriverTask: per-peer counter in HashMap<String, u32>, insertion into locked_peers HashSet on third strike + WrongPeer telemetry, short-circuit in handle_control::Start path.

EphemeralKeySigner is test/development only — it generates a fresh Ed25519 keypair unbound to the Matrix device identity. Production MUST use a Matrix-backed signer (mxdx-btk follow-up); inline doc warns.
IdleWatchdog uses a fresh tokio::time::sleep(window) each loop iteration after a Reset, effectively restarting the timer. Tested with tokio::test(start_paused=true) + advance + yield_many helper.
SDP fingerprint extraction canonicalizes to uppercase with colons; multiple fingerprints (RTP+RTCP split) resolved by lex-smallest — both peers apply the same rule so transcripts match.
ed25519-dalek added as a shared dep (not native-only) — pure Rust, wasm-compatible. Future wasm P2PTransport (Phase 8) reuses the same verify.rs module.
